claim_boundary.next_gate: Run C4/C5 same-access denominator comparison before C6 leakage-free trace, C7 machine-check bundle, or any B7 ledger retest.
claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: R59 does not prove a global O3 theorem, does not compare against a same-access denominator, does not audit leakage, does not produce a machine-check replay bundle, and does not grant reroute or B7/STV credit.
claim_boundary.what_is_supported: R59 completes the restricted C3 same-unitary replay certificate for the 8 O3-F4 single-qubit RZ rows accepted by R58, adds negative-control perturbations that the verifier rejects, and normalizes the observed R58 evidence-packet file-SHA staleness by rebinding actual file hashes.

bundle.artifact: R59 C3 all-row same-unitary replay certificate bundle
bundle.claim_boundary: R59 completes C3 for the restricted O3-F4 single-qubit RZ replay surface. It also records that R58 evidence-packet file SHA fields are stale after final row binding, while semantic packet hashes still match. C4/C5, C6, C7, O3 closure, reroute, and B7 ledger credit remain open.

requirements.label: R58 upstream accepted all 8 C2 source-backed discriminator rows with zero B7 credit
R59 parses exactly one OpenQASM 3.0 RZ angle for each source and candidate row
All critical R58-bound files hash-match and evidence packets remain semantically bound
Positive same-unitary replay certificates pass for all 8 rows
Negative-control perturbations are rejected for all 8 rows
R59 completes the restricted C3 replay certificate without promoting O3/reroute/B7 credit
R59 leaves C4/C5, C6, C7, and B7 ledger retest open
R59 bundle and per-row certificates are hash-bound

title: B1/B7 Cone01 R59 O3-F4 C3 Same-Unitary Replay Certificate Gate
